title: Decisions, statements & accounts
summary: This is a navigational overview page on the ECB website explaining the institution's monetary policy framework, including the three key policy rates and the broader toolkit of instruments available to the Governing Council. The page does not contain specific decisions, statements, or recent macro catalyst events—it serves as institutional background content.
key_points: ECB's three policy rates are: deposit facility rate, main refinancing operations rate, and marginal lending facility rate
Since March 2024, the Governing Council steers monetary policy stance through the deposit facility rate
Short-term money market rates are expected to evolve in vicinity of deposit facility rate with tolerance for volatility
ECB policy tools include: central bank loans against collateral at fixed rates, negative interest rates, TLTROs, asset purchases, and forward guidance
The page references a strategy review but provides no specific details about outcomes
No current interest rate values or recent decisions are provided in this page
why_it_matters: This page provides foundational context on ECB monetary policy operations but does not itself constitute a market-moving event or catalyst. It is reference material explaining how ECB tools work rather than a specific policy decision or release.
macro_context: The page explains that ECB monetary policy tools influence the amount and cost of loans to affect financing conditions and economic activity in the euro area, ultimately targeting 2% inflation. No current macro data or policy decisions are presented.
market_structure_context: Describes the transmission mechanism from policy rates (deposit facility, MRO, marginal lending facility) to short-term money market rates. Notes that post-financial crisis, ECB expanded tools when short-term rates approached the effective lower bound.
